description: The NumericOverrideGather component acts the same as a ValueUserOverride`1 with the key difference being it can get the average, minimum, maximum, and sum of all the values users picked that are still in the session.

fields.Default: The default value to drive ``Target`` with on the user's local machine if they don't have an override in the list of ``_overrides``.
fields.CreateOverrideOnWrite: Whether changes to ``Target`` creates a new override or updates the overrides value for that user.
fields.PersistentOverrides: Whether the ``_overrides`` list contents are saved when this component is saved.
fields._overrides: A list of overrides for different users.
fields.Target: The field to drive with different values for each user's local machine using the ``_overrides`` list.
fields.Min: The minimum value of all the ``_overrides`` for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of ``Default``.
fields.Max: The maximum value of all the ``_overrides`` for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of ``Default``.
fields.Sum: The value for if of all the ``_overrides`` were added together for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of ``Default``.
fields.Average: The average value of all the ``_overrides`` for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of ``Default``.
fields.ExcludeHeadless: Whether headless client values should be excluded from calculations.

usage: Can be used to check if all users are using the same setting (like saying they're ready for a game round to start) or check the average of what people say the number of jellybeans in a jar is (so that someone can guess the average and get the closest to the answer)
